programme.title: Spitfire
programme.desc: The story of the British single-seat fighter plane the Supermarine Spitfire, designed by RJ Mitchell and famous for its role in the Battle of Britain. Archive footage, new film of the aircraft in flight and interviews with the last surviving veterans who flew the plane in combat combine to tell its story.

programme.title: When We Were Kings
programme.desc: Leon Gast's Oscar-winning documentary charting the challenge of an ageing Muhammad Ali for George Foreman's World Heavyweight title held in Zaire in 1974. The film explores the impact of Ali's visit to Africa before focusing on the epic fight, dubbed the 'Rumble in the Jungle', which has gone down in history as one of the greatest of all time. Spike Lee and Norman Mailer are among the contributors

programme.title: Queen of Speed
programme.desc: A look at the career of French driver Michele Mouton, charting her battle to rise to the top of the male-dominated world of rallying in the 1970s and '80s.

programme.title: SIC: Prince of MotoGP
programme.desc: Profile of MotoGP rider Marco Simoncelli, made to mark the 10th anniversary of his death.

programme.title: Chernobyl: My Promised Land
programme.desc: Eastern Ukrainian refugees find themselves unwelcome in the village of Dytyatky near Chernobyl, with locals sceptical about their leader's madcap business ventures.

programme.title: Discovering: Hugh Grant
programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the English actor, best-known for his roles in Love Actually, Notting Hill and Four Weddings and a Funeral.

programme.title: Rolling Stone: Stories from the Edge
programme.desc: Exploring the unique relationship between John Lennon and the magazine, and how coverage of president Richard Nixon's re-election campaign disrupted political journalism.

programme.title: Discovering: Leonardo DiCaprio
programme.desc: A profile of the American actor, who rose to fame through his roles in Romeo + Juliet and Titanic, and won a Best Actor Oscar for The Revenant.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: The life and work of American director David Fincher, whose films include Alien 3, Seven, The Game, Fight Club, The Social Network and Gone Girl.

programme.title: The Movies
programme.desc: The journey through American cinema continues with more from the 1990s, including Unforgiven, Heat, Fargo, Toy Story, Groundhog Day and Titanic.

programme.title: Burden of Proof: Who Killed Jennifer Pandos?
programme.desc: The case of a 15-year-old who went missing in 1987, with her brother's investigation leading him to become strongly convinced his parents were implicated.

programme.title: The Seventies
programme.desc: The documentary examines how America went from bad to worse as it dealt with one crisis after another throughout the presidential terms of Gerald Ford and Jimmy Carter.

programme.title: Hawking: Can You Hear Me?
programme.desc: The life and career of Stephen Hawking, exploring the remarkable story of a man who became one of the most famous physicists of the 20th century.

programme.title: I Am Raquel Welch
programme.desc: Documentary celebrating the life of a trailblazing actress who not only redefined the idea of a Hollywood sex symbol, but also led the way for modern-day action heroines.

programme.title: My Icon: Denise Lewis
programme.desc: The former British heptathlete and Olympic gold medallist speaks about the figures who have been an inspiration over the course of her life.

programme.title: I Am Johnny Cash
programme.desc: A celebration of the country singer-songwriter built around 12 of his classic tracks spanning four decades, and featuring interviews with his family and friends. Featuring contributions from Sheryl Crow, Kris Kristofferson, Willie Nelson, John Mellencamp and Kid Rock.

programme.title: My Icon: Thierry Henry
programme.desc: The former Arsenal and France forward speaks about the individuals that have been an inspiration to him over the course of his career.

programme.title: Bruno v Tyson
programme.desc: The story of the boxing rivalry between Frank Bruno and Mike Tyson, spanning two epic fights marred by politics and volatile race relations, with the old foes coming together for a reunion.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A close-up view of sex, bug-style, as David Attenborough talks viewers through the different ways in which creepy-crawlies reproduce. Size matters for the minuscule male orb spider, creepily sneaking up on its intended and trying to mate without her noticing, while there's no rest for the lothario-like butterfly, which has plenty of notches on its proverbial bedpost. However, the harvestman spider

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his close-up examination of the creepy-crawly world, shifting focus to the bugs' ultimate invention - the colony. Key residents of these minuscule mega-cities include honey bees, termites, leafcutter ants - who have a knack for farming - and their Argentine cousins, members of a community so vast it spans an entire continent.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Documentary following daily life at Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Dorset, where staff look after monkeys, chimps, orangutans and other primates.

programme.title: Wolves and Lions: Old Enemies
programme.desc: Felines and canids clash across the world. This edition dives into direct battles and strategies to steal food. Intelligence and strength decide who reigns in each territory.

programme.title: Wild Sky
programme.desc: Examining the Amazon rainforest's oxygen-breathing trees and following flamingos that thrive in the sun's scorching UV rays.

programme.title: Islands
programme.desc: Hyenas claim the largest island in the delta, but must defend it. Elephants and hippos sculpt the waterways and make new homes for smaller creatures.

programme.title: Islands
programme.desc: Never-before-filmed highland leopards stalk Sambar deer, while territorial mudskippers and fiddler crabs compete for homes and mates.

programme.title: Ocean Parks
programme.desc: Profiles of the world's most spectacular marine habitats, beginning with the Caribbean island of Bonaire, a leading destination for eco-tourists.

programme.title: America's Wild Border: Northern Exposure
programme.desc: The animals that live on the border between the US and Canada face many challenges - even politics. This documentary reveals how the boundary affects them over 12 months.

programme.title: Wild Sky

programme.title: Matsalu Moose: Wild Giants of the Baltics
programme.desc: Matsalu National Park at the Baltic Sea is known for being one of Europe's most important stopover places for migrating birds. However, few know that an exceptionally large moose colony also lives here. This documentary follows the moose through the eyes of two yearlings as they are chased away from their mother before she gives birth to the next calf.

programme.title: Kingdom of the Tide
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at the intertidal zone, the point at which the ocean meets the land, and the struggle for survival facing the creatures that inhabit this realm. Their success is critical, because this is one of the most important habitats on the planet - a vital connection between worlds.

programme.title: Great Lakes Wild
programme.desc: A research team travels to the uninhabited island of Isle Royale in Lake Superior, which has been a battleground between moose and wolves since the early 20th century.

programme.title: Great Lakes Wild

programme.title: Gladiators
programme.desc: Documentary revealing the secrets and ingenious tactics of nature's toughest fighters in their battles for survival. Narrated by Sean Bean.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: This edition celebrates the new Orangutan Jungle School graduates and cameras follow the convoy to the release site where the team bids their friends a poignant farewell.

programme.title: Shark Squad
programme.desc: Leading shark ecologist Mauricio Hoyos attempts a TV first as he attaches a camera to the dorsal fin of a great white shark off Guadalupe Island.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: A hyena goes in search of a new clan, a challenge that will require all its strength, intelligence and cunning.

programme.title: Airborne
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the challenges and freedom of animals that use the air to survive and thrive, with the opening edition demonstrating how different species use air, from rookies to the masters of the skies.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and his crew help a newlywed couple turn a wedding gift into an alpine ski bungalow; Chase and his team gut the camp and start from scratch to transform it into a beautiful bungalow that the newlyweds will enjoy for generations to come.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase gets a call from retired U.S. Army Staff Sgt. Travis Mills, who is building a retreat to help combat-injured veterans and their families; the builders must turn a cottage into an ADA-compliant event center for veterans and their families.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A couple's Belle Island camp has a long, multi-generational history within their family spanning nearly 100 years, and they call on the Cabin Masters to bring it into the present day while preserving it for the next generation.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Builder Chase Morrill and his team tackle a family camp on the coast of the New Meadows River in West Bath, Maine; the third-generation owners have decided to add more appropriate living quarters for the large family reunions they host each year.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the team are challenged to take a former garage and turn it into a home that will comfortably accommodate six siblings and their 17 kids; they race to convert the cramped camp into a real cabin the family can enjoy for years to come.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the team are hired to finish a cabin for a homeowner who's been collecting materials for years but hasn't been able to finish the project; with a budget of $20,000, the crew hopes to make the homeowners' dream a reality in eight weeks.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The team helps Matthew Dix restore his grandfather's old shed to its former glory; with Dixie's grandmother waiting impatiently next door, the crew members visit every chance they get to put hammer to nails to restore the decrepit cabin.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Chase and the crew are tasked with the relocation and land manifestation of a yurt on an island on Togus Pond; the team seeks to beat the ice and snow so they can have the yurt ready for the family to drop their fishing lines in the frozen pond.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Chadwick family has purchased a cabin built in 1906 and need the Cabin Masters help for a remodel; with an eight-week deadline, the team must fix a rotting ceiling, update the interior and create a custom outdoor bar and fire pit.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Cabin Masters are summoned to the medieval roleplaying community of Burgundar to repair their market, fire pit, and gathering inn; a ferocious rainstorm and winter weather threaten to thwart the team's grand plans for the camp.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family enlists Chase and the team to update their 30-year-old cabin, bunkhouse and boat house in Lincolnville, Maine; the team faces surprises around every corner as they race to finish the renovations in time for the family to enjoy the summer.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A sudden severe thunderstorm destroyed the Palumbo family's beloved camp, but they're ready to rebuild; the team hopes to grant them a miracle in the wreckage, with a bigger bathroom and a sunroom that feels connected to the rest of the camp.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders restore a timber frame home on a frozen field in West Virginia; Mark and Johnny visit an extraordinary mountain top retreat with a log home and timber frame pool house; Alex Webb builds a lord's table and bench at his workshop.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The Barnwood Builders travel to Kentucky to help Sherman Thompson build a fishing cabin for his family; with help from Sherman's daughter, Felicia, they stack a cabin with sawn logs, and all that hard work leads up to a big reward for Sherman.

programme.title: Human vs Hamster
programme.desc: Lawyers and wrestlers step into the ring against familiar foes and the new furry competitor, the Professor.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara seeks to transform a multi-family 1892 brick Craftsman into a single-family dwelling while keeping its original details; she and her father attempt to recover a vintage pocket door trapped inside a wall and update the home's electricity.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara continues working on an 1892 Craftsman and struggles to add a master bathroom; then, she finds a way to reuse floor boards as a porch swing, but her plan to reuse a claw-foot bathtub hits a snag because of a narrow doorway.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara Day works on restoring a 1909 Craftsman home, opening up its meandering layout and ripping out the tacky additions that have stripped the house of its original charm; she saves vintage light switches and a wallpaper mural.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara continues renovating a 1909 Craftsman home, transforming a small bedroom into the master bath; she refurbishes French doors, vintage sinks and a tub; during the walk-through, Tamara discovers the creepiest thing she's ever found in a home.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara annexes an oversized dining room to enlarge a tiny kitchen in a 1926 English Tudor; she rips out a tacky mantel to create a stunning fireplace surround; using a piece of reclaimed walnut, she creates a large desktop for an in-home office.

programme.title: Bargain Mansions
programme.desc: Tamara Day struggles to create a more spacious master suite until deciding to add a second deck to a 1926 English Tudor; Tamara strips off numerous coats of paint to restore original doors; she adds an outdoor living area with a fire pit.
